description: In an apartment living room, Chandler stands between Eddie and Joey, tossing a small ball between his hands. "Wow," Chandler says.
Chandler gestures toward Eddie with one hand. "You know, we got the talking and he said he needed a place and I had a spare room." Joey keeps his eyes on Chandler.
"Oh, now it's a spare room?" Joey asks.
"Well, yeah, in that it's not being used and I have it to spare," Chandler replies, looking back at Joey.
Joey looks down toward his jacket, while Chandler and Eddie stand quietly.
Joey pulls a hat shaped like a moose head from his jacket. "Well, I got what I came for."
Joey places the moose hat onto his head. Chandler and Eddie look at him in silence.
Joey turns toward the door. "I'll see you guys."
Joey takes a step toward the doorway.
"All hey, Joe, when'd you start using mousse in your hair?" Chandler asks. Joey stops in the doorway.
Eddie laughs loudly and points at Joey. Joey turns back to face them with a neutral expression.
"This guy great," Eddie says, patting Chandler's shoulder.
"Or" Eddie continues speaking, leaving his hand resting on Chandler's shoulder.
Joey continues looking at them from the doorway.
"Yeah, he is," Chandler replies, his volume lower as he avoids eye contact with Joey.
Joey turns away and walks out the door. The setting changes to a view of city buildings under daylight.
The scene changes to a different apartment where Ross, Rachel, and Monica sit together on a couch.
Rachel eats from a bowl of popcorn. "I can't believe he has a new roommate. Who is this guy?"
"Eddie something. You just met him. It'll never last," Ross replies.
Rachel continues chewing her popcorn.
"He's just a rebound roommate," Monica says, holding a glass.
Phoebe opens the apartment door and walks quickly inside, holding a black videotape.
"Hey," Phoebe says.
Phoebe walks further into the living room.
"Oh, check it out. Oh, check it out. It's Smelly Cat. The video," Phoebe says, holding up the tape.
Phoebe walks to the television set and inserts the tape into the VCR.
"No. Okay, I haven't seen it yet, so if you don't like it well, so what? None of you ever made a video?" Phoebe says, gesturing with her free hand as she addresses the group.
Ross, Rachel, and Monica watch her in silence. Phoebe grabs a remote and sits on the arm of a nearby chair.
"Hey," Phoebe presses a button on the remote, pointing it at the television.
A black-and-white music video plays on the television screen, displaying the text "Smelly Cat" before showing an older woman opening a door.
"Get out of here. Can you stay back?" the woman in the video yells, pointing her finger forward.
The video shows a fluffy cat eating off a newspaper on the ground.
The screen cuts to Phoebe in the video, wearing a gown and standing in mist. Monica, Ross, and Rachel watch from the couch.
Phoebe on the couch says, "Look at me." On the television, an instrumental track plays as Phoebe sings, "Smelly cat,"
Phoebe sings in the music video. Ross and Rachel watch the screen, their eyebrows drawn together.
The group on the couch remains silent, maintaining their gaze on the television.
"Smelly cat, smelly cat it's not your fault," Phoebe sings on the television while holding the cat in her arms.
Ross, Rachel, and Monica continue watching the screen with stiff posture.
"Smelly, smelly, smelly, really bad, it's not your fault, Smelly cat." Three women in the music video perform as backup singers.
The video cuts back to Phoebe holding the cat.
"Oh my God," Phoebe on the couch gasps, bringing her hands near her face.
"I know. I sound amazing," Phoebe says with a wide smile, moving her hands in front of her.
Ross and Rachel glance at each other. Monica shifts her eyes away from the screen.
"We'll take you to the vet. You're obviously not the favorite pet." The video shows several people standing around a burning barrel in an alley.
"Smelly Cat." The backup singers chant in the video.
Phoebe in the music video sings and sways her arms.
"It's not your fault." The singers on the television continue.
Phoebe in the video holds the cat. Phoebe on the couch continues to watch.
"I've never heard myself sing before. I mean, except in my own head," Phoebe on the couch states.
"Oh, this is so cool. Now I can hear what you hear," Phoebe continues.
Monica looks slightly downward, raising her eyebrows.
"Pretty different," Monica remarks softly.
Phoebe leans back comfortably on the chair arm.
"I am sorry, but I'm incredibly talented," Phoebe states, sitting up straight.
Ross, Rachel, and Monica remain quiet, staring ahead.
"It's not your" The backup singers on the television continue their verse.
The scene transitions to an exterior shot of the Twin Towers at dusk.
Joey approaches an apartment door in a hallway and turns the knob.
Joey enters the apartment, where Ross is cooking at the stove and Chandler is seated with a newspaper.
"What's going on, man?" Ross turns from the stove and speaks to Joey.
Joey walks forward into the kitchen area.
"Eddie," Joey says, looking around.
"Morning," Joey says, looking at Chandler.
"Morning," Chandler replies, briefly looking up from his paper.
"I just came by to pick up my mail," Joey says, pointing his hand toward a table.
Joey walks over toward the kitchen counter.
Joey asks, "Where's the mail?" Chandler points toward a different table and replies, "Oh, it's over there on the table."
Joey turns his head toward the table Chandler indicated.
"You don't keep it over here on this table anymore?" Joey asks. "No, Eddie likes to keep it over there," Chandler answers.
Joey looks ahead silently with a steady gaze.
In an apartment kitchen and living area, Chandler sits at the counter reading. Near the front of the room, Joey bends down holding a black hose. Eddie stands by the stove.
Eddie approaches the counter holding a skillet and a plate. He scrapes food onto the plate and says, "All right. Here you go, my friend. Eggs a la."

Chandler takes a bite using chopsticks, nods, and makes an audible "mmm" sound as brief audience laughter is heard. Joey stands up in the living area and begins taking off his black leather coat, showing a brown jacket underneath.
Joey walks toward the kitchen counter, holding his black coat, and looks at Chandler and Eddie.
Chandler looks at Eddie and says, "That's great."
Joey stops near the counter. Eddie turns to look at him.
Joey says, "Must. No, I just thought you liked your eggs with the bread with the hole in the middle..."
"...a la me," Joey finishes, gesturing to himself. Chandler turns his gaze to Joey as audience laughter sounds.
Chandler points his chopsticks toward Eddie.
"Well, I do, but Eddie makes them this way and, well, they're pretty darn good," Chandler says.
Eddie holds up the skillet toward Joey and says, "Try some on that's extra." Joey shakes his head, replying, "No, thanks," followed by audience laughter.
Eddie lowers the skillet and turns toward the counter.
Eddie picks up a glass of juice, drinks it quickly, sets it down, and says, "All right, well, you guys, I'm out of here," walking away as audience laughter plays.
Eddie grabs a black coat from the sofa. Joey and Chandler watch him.
Eddie walks past them and says, "See you, pals." Chandler replies, "See ya."
Eddie exits through the front door. Joey stands by the counter, hands near his pockets, watching the door, then turns his gaze to Chandler.
Joey looks at Chandler and asks, "So how are you two getting along?"
Chandler gestures with his chopsticks and says, "Oh, I couldn't be happier." Joey nods and says, "Great." Audience laughter is heard.
Joey looks down, grabs a carton of orange juice, and says, "Well, I'm happy for you," pouring juice into a glass.
Joey tips the carton completely upside down over his glass, but only a few drops fall out. He stares at the empty carton while audience laughter sounds.
Joey sets the empty carton on the counter with a loud thud and says, "All right, that's it."
Gesturing broadly with his arms, Joey says, "He just comes in here, mr. Johnny Neweggs with" as audience laughter plays.
Chandler watches silently as Joey stands with his arms extended.
Joey points toward the door, saying, "His moving the mail and his see your pals," gesturing outward.
Chandler keeps his eyes on Joey, his lips slightly parted.
Joey points at the empty carton and says, "And now there's no juice. There's no juice for the people who need the juice..."
"...and want the juice. I need the juice," Joey finishes, his volume louder than his previous sentence, accompanied by audience laughter.
Chandler looks across the counter toward the sink area.
Chandler extends his arm, pointing across the room, and says, "There's another carton right over there," followed by audience laughter.
Joey looks in the direction Chandler pointed.
Joey turns back to Chandler and asks, "Hey, this isn't about juice anymore, right, man?"
Chandler raises his hands, asking, "All right, so what's it about?" Joey immediately replies, "Eggs," drawing audience laughter.
Joey steps closer to Chandler, lowering his arms.
"Whose eggs do you like better, his or mine?" Joey asks. Chandler answers, "Well, I like both eggs equally," as audience laughter is heard.
"Oh, come on," Joey says, turning his body slightly away.
Joey points his hand toward Chandler and says, "Nobody likes two different kinds of eggs equally. You like one better than the other, and I want to know which." Chandler begins, "Well, what's..."
"...the difference? Your eggs aren't here anymore, are they?" Chandler continues, gesturing with his hand.
Chandler maintains eye contact with Joey, his hand resting near the counter.
Pointing toward the door, Chandler says, "You took your eggs and you left." Audience laughter sounds.
Chandler lowers his hand and watches Joey.
Chandler pulls his arms back and asks, "Did you really expect me to never find new eggs?" followed by audience laughter.
Joey looks away briefly, shifting his weight.
Joey shakes his head slightly, looks back at Chandler, and says, "Well, maybe this isn't about the eggs."
Joey stands still, keeping his gaze on Chandler.
Gesturing with his hand, Joey says, "Maybe this is about the chicken that laid them." Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler stares silently at Joey. Joey stands still with his hands resting at his sides.
Chandler leans forward slightly and asks, "What chicken?"
Joey raises his hand and says, "There's no chicken," as audience laughter plays.
Joey stands in silence, looking directly at Chandler.
Throwing his hand down, Joey turns away and says, "I can't talk to you," drawing audience laughter.

Joey walks to the door and exits the apartment. Chandler remains still, watching him leave. The setting changes to a different apartment living room. Rachel sits at a table applying nail polish. Monica stands near a television in the background, and Ross sits on a sofa.
Ross holds a remote control and says, "I want to watch Entertainment tonight." Monica, standing behind the sofa, replies, "Tough." Audience laughter sounds.
Ross raises his hands holding the remote and says, "Noogies. We're watching Predators of the Serengeti," as brief audience laughter is heard.
Monica remains standing behind the sofa, looking at Ross.
Monica says, "You great."
Rachel points her nail polish brush and says, "Stop it. It's my TV."
Rachel continues applying nail polish while Ross and Monica look toward the television.
Monica says, "Quit it." Ross quickly replies, "Bite me."

Rachel covers her face with her hands, saying, "Oh, my God." Ross points the remote over his shoulder toward Monica and says, "Well, my nugget keeps changing the channel," followed by audience laughter.
Monica gestures toward Ross and says, "Oh, that's great. Why don't you tell Mommy on me?" Rachel stands up from the table, saying, "Now..." as audience laughter plays.
"...I'm Mommy in this little play," Rachel says, touching her chest. She points forward, saying, "All right, look, I refuse to get sucked into this like..."
"...weird little Geller," Rachel continues, gesturing broadly as she walks around the table toward Ross and Monica.
"...dimension thing, okay?" Rachel says, pointing up. She turns toward the hallway, saying, "So I'm going to go and take a nice long, hot bubble bath..."
"...because you kids are driving me crazy," she finishes, waving her hand backward as she walks away, accompanied by audience laughter.
Ross sits on the sofa looking down. Upbeat instrumental music begins playing. The faces of Ross, Joey, Monica, Chandler, and Rachel appear briefly in rapid succession.
In a living room, Ross sits on a couch holding a TV remote while Monica stands facing him.
Monica says, "Okay? What are we going to do about this?" Ross replies, "Well, I guess we could tape entertainment tonight."
Monica gestures broadly and says, "Not that. This. US. Oh, my God,"
Monica points at Ross and says, "Ross."
Ross raises a hand, palm out, looking at Monica as audience laughter plays.
Monica points at Ross and says, "Ever since you've been"
Ross continues holding his hand up defensively.
Ross says, "You've gone Ultrasonic again, right?"
Monica stomps her foot.
Monica says, "I just can't stand you being here all the" before Ross asks, "why?"
Ross spreads his arms and asks, "Why can't you stand me being here?"
Ross says, "We're just having fun."
Monica steps closer, saying, "Fun? You think this is fun?"
Ross nods slightly.
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "Yeah, come on. I mean, I thought we're just fooling around like when we were kids."
Monica leans forward slightly and says, "Ross, I hated you when we were kids."
Ross stares at Monica in silence as audience laughter plays.
Ross asks, "You hated me when we were kids?"
Monica gestures with her hands and says, "Yes, I hated you. I mean, I loved you and you're my brother, so I have to kind of way."
Monica continues, "But basically, yeah, I hated your guts."
Ross looks down briefly, then back up.
Ross asks, "Why did you hate me?"
Monica replies, "Because you were mean to me and you teased me and you always, always"
Monica finishes, "Got your way."
Ross looks at Monica.
Ross gestures with the remote, asking, "And that wasn't fun for you?"
Monica shakes her head as audience laughter plays.
Monica throws her arms up, saying, "Taha,"
Ross looks down, his expression still.
Ross says softly, "I can't believe you hated me."
Monica's gaze stays on Ross.
Monica steps toward the couch, saying, "But now I love you,"
Monica steps closer and leans over the coffee table toward Ross.
Monica says, "And not just because I have to." Ross looks up and asks, "Really?" Monica replies, "Yeah."
Monica stays leaned forward, looking at Ross.
Monica points at Ross and says, "You're just going to have to stop pissing me off."
Ross looks up at Monica, then nods slightly as audience laughter plays.
Ross says, "I can do that." Monica extends a hand to him and says, "Then I won't have to kill you."
Ross takes her hand and they shake hands.
Ross says, "So you want to watch"
Monica moves around the coffee table to sit beside Ross.
Ross points the remote at the television and finishes, "Entertainment tonight?" Monica sits down and says, "Thanks."
A female anchor appears on a television screen.
The television anchor says, "Tonight, America's premier sports magazine moves to prime you know what the biggest names in all the games." Ross and Monica sit together on the couch.
Ross looks at Monica, and says, "If you really want to watch"
Ross points the remote again, and says, "that Serengeti thing, we can" Monica smiles and replies, "oh, okay,"
Ross points the remote toward the television as Monica leans back slightly.

The setting changes to a nighttime view of a city skyline with illuminated bridges and skyscrapers. Then, inside a coffee shop, Ross sits on a couch with Rachel resting against him, while Monica stands nearby. Phoebe walks toward them.
Phoebe touches her chest and says, "Listen. You are not going to believe this,"
Phoebe continues, "But that is not me singing on the video."
Rachel and Monica look at Phoebe as audience laughter plays.
Rachel says, "No."
Phoebe nods her head.
Phoebe says, "Yes." Monica asks, "How did you find out?"
Phoebe gestures and says, "Well, okay, the record company sent over this piece of paper for me to sign saying that it's okay for someone"
Phoebe gestures with one hand and finishes, "Else to sing for me. That was my first clue."
Monica places her hands on her hips as audience laughter plays.
Monica asks, "So what are you going to do?" Phoebe gestures and says, "Well, I can't work with people who would do this." Rachel says, "Sure."
Phoebe says, "I mean, this poor woman."
Rachel looks up at Phoebe with a furrowed brow.
Ross asks, "What woman?"
Phoebe replies, "The voicewoman."
Phoebe continues, "I mean, she has a great voice, but she doesn't have a video."
Monica leans forward on the back of the couch and asks, "Okay, Phoebe, but what about you?"
Phoebe points to herself, saying, "Well, I have a video. You have to pay attention."
Rachel and Ross look at Phoebe as audience laughter plays.
Phoebe gestures with her hands and says, "No, this voice woman, she's so talented,"
Phoebe continues, "But according to the producer people, she doesn't have the right look or"
Phoebe drops her hands and finishes, "Something."
Phoebe raises her hands again.
Phoebe says, "She's like one of those animals at the pound who nobody wants because they're not pretty enough."
Phoebe adds, "Or it's like some old dog"
Phoebe gestures outward.
Phoebe says, "Who's just kind of, like, stinky and oh my God, she's smelly."
Phoebe touches her chest, keeping her eyes wide as audience laughter plays.
In a coffee shop, Monica, Rachel, and Ross sit on a sofa. Phoebe sits on the backrest, and Chandler stands behind the sofa, leaning forward. Monica says, "Oh."
Phoebe says, "Oh, that song has so many levels."
The setting changes to an apartment living room. Eddie sits on a sofa, reading a book. Chandler walks out from the kitchen area.
Chandler points his thumb toward an area off-screen and asks, "Hey, Eddie, you want to play some?"
Eddie keeps his gaze on his book.
Eddie briefly looks up from his book and says, "No, thanks, man."
Eddie returns his gaze to the book, adding, "I'm not really into sports."
Chandler stands silently, his lips pressed together. Audience laughter plays.
Chandler nods slightly, saying, "Yeah, okay. All right." He then turns away.
Chandler takes a step back, briefly raising his hands out to his sides.
Chandler turns and walks energetically toward a recliner chair, holding a remote control. "Doesn't matter. Time for Baywatch," he says.
Eddie turns his head toward Chandler. Audience laughter is audible.
Eddie asks, "You like that show?"
Chandler pauses and turns back toward Eddie, his mouth slightly open.
Chandler asks, "You don't like that show?"
Eddie shakes his head slightly.
Eddie says, "No, man. I mean, it's just a bunch of pretty people running around on the beach."
Chandler points at Eddie and says, "Well, that's the brilliance of it."
Chandler lowers himself onto the arm of the recliner. Audience laughter plays.
Chandler gestures with his hand and says, "The pretty people,"
Chandler holds his hand gesture, looking at Eddie.
Chandler drops his hand and says, "They're running."
Audience laughter plays as Eddie closes his book and prepares to stand.
Eddie stands up, saying, "I'm going to go read in my room for a little while," and begins walking away.
Chandler watches him go, briefly extending his hand.
Chandler says, "Okay. Man."
A television screen displays two women running on a beach in black swimsuits. Upbeat music plays.
The setting changes to Joey's apartment. Joey sits in a black recliner, aiming a remote at the television and smiling. An empty matching recliner sits next to him.
Joey's smile fades. He lowers the remote and turns his gaze toward the empty recliner. A slow song with vocals begins to play.
Joey looks down at the remote in his hands as the song lyrics play: "I think of all the friends I've known you".
Joey lifts the remote to his chin, his expression remaining still.
Joey wraps his arms around himself as the song lyrics continue: "When I die to tell the phone".
The setting shifts back to Chandler's apartment, where Chandler is playing foosball by himself.
Chandler leans over the foosball table as the sung lyric "Nobody" is heard. He scores a goal against himself and stands up quickly.
Chandler lowers his head, his shoulders slumping.
The setting changes to Joey's apartment, where he stands alone at a mini ping-pong table. He hits the ball, and it bounces off the raised half of the table.
The setting changes back to Chandler's apartment. He sits on a desk in front of a window, looking outside. Rain streaks the glass.
Chandler continues staring out the rain-streaked window, resting his chin against his arm.
Joey is seen behind a pane of glass streaked with running water. The song lyrics play: "Don't wanna live".
Joey continues staring through the wet glass.
Joey rests his chin on his hand as the sung lyric "By my" is heard.
Joey is seen resting his head on a dining table, looking through a decorative glass water feature in his living room.
Joey maintains his gaze through the running water.
The screen becomes black, and the song ends. An exterior view of a city street appears, followed by a return to the coffee shop. Inside, Phoebe sits on a stool holding a guitar.
Phoebe strums her guitar and says, "Start off with a little song called Smelly Cat".
Phoebe smiles, and audience laughter is heard.
Phoebe holds up one hand and speaks into the microphone, "Oh, for those of you who are wondering, this is my real voice."
Joey, Monica, and Chandler are seated together on the sofa, watching Phoebe.
Phoebe strums her guitar and sings, "Smelly cat, smelly cat what are they feeding you?"
She calls out, "Everybody?"
The group sings, "Smelly cat, smelly cat it's not your fault".
Phoebe points and says, "Monica," and Monica sings, "they won't take you".
Monica finishes, "To the vet". Phoebe says, "Chandler".
Chandler looks at Phoebe, hesitating briefly before preparing to sing.
Chandler sings, "Obviously not their favorite pet". Phoebe says, "Joey," and Joey sings, "May not be a bed of roses".
Phoebe says, "Rachel," and Rachel sings, "You're".
Rachel finishes singing, "Not friend to those with noses". Phoebe says, "Ross,"
Ross responds, "Those are the only lines we have. Sorry."
Phoebe nods and says, "Okay, back to the chorus, everybody."
She begins to sing, "Smelly cat, smelly cat what".
The group sings along: "Are they feeding you?"
They continue singing, "Smelly cat, smelly cat it's not your fault."
The group holds the final note.
An off-screen voice sings, "asthma".
The group on the sofa laughs and applauds.
An off-screen voice sings, "feeding you asthmatic".
The group continues to smile and applaud.
An off-screen voice sings, "It's not your fault it's."
Ross rests his chin on Rachel's shoulder as they smile.
White copyright and production text is visible against a darkened background where seated figures are faintly discernible. The text gradually disappears. Audience applause is audible.
The screen becomes completely dark. The audience applause fades to silence.
A music video begins, showing Phoebe against a hazy, purplish background. An upbeat instrumental track plays. Looking directly forward, Phoebe sings, "Smelly cat, smelly cat."
